what_it_does: Acceptance test for the R1.2 render-declaration layer: it validates the typed registry, checks that every documented stream shape maps to the right render key, runs real T2 stream captures and a real-transcript sample for full declared coverage, exercises the loud undeclared and family-fallback paths, and spins up the supervisor service with a stub Claude binary to verify every stub emit is fanned as a declared event on /watch and that unknown emits surface as agent_sessions.render_drop in events.jsonl.
observations: Uses real captures under ~/xsession-tests and a deterministic sample from ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl.
Includes an inline stub Claude script (RICH_STUB) that drives the supervisor through one full turn.
Uses a global PASS counter and a check() helper that asserts and increments.
Tests invalid registry loud failure by writing a bad registry to a tempfile and restoring the real cache afterward.
Verifies field truncation with a visible __truncated marker for fields over 64KB.
Checks AskUserQuestion nuance where blocks_execution is true.
Registers and removes a private _DROP_HOOKS entry to test gap telemetry.
Polls HTTP endpoints and events.jsonl with timeouts during the service test.
standouts: Service-level end-to-end acceptance using a stub Claude binary and live /watch stream inspection.
Real-data coverage assertion over captured stdout and sampled transcript files.
Explicit loud undeclared path: unknown emits still render as UnknownEvent and fire drop hooks.
Large dispatch matrix covering 40+ documented event shapes.
conventions: Plain main()-driven test runner rather than pytest.
Heavy module-level docstring stating the proof claims.
Hardcoded test port and environment-variable-driven supervisor configuration.
Uses tempfile, subprocess, urllib.request, and random.Random with a fixed seed.
concerns: Hardcoded local paths (~/xsession-tests, ~/.claude/projects) make the test environment-dependent.
Global PASS counter and side-effect hooks on a shared registry module.
Service test relies on polling sleeps and fixed timeouts, which can be flaky.
Direct access to rd._DROP_HOOKS private attribute.
Large inline stub script is hard to maintain and duplicates emit shape knowledge.
No explicit cleanup of the temporary service directory.
notes: A broad acceptance harness that tries to prove the render-declaration registry covers every real and stub emit shape and that gaps are loudly visible.
questions: What is the exact JSON schema of the runtime.render_declaration registry?
How does render_one decide truthiness and what does it return?
What is the contract of /watch and the shape of events.jsonl drop records?
Are the real capture paths expected only on a specific developer machine?
How is the supervisor expected to handle the stub binary's lifecycle and port binding?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring claims it proves registry validity, dispatch matrix, real data coverage, loud undeclared handling, and service fan-out, and the code exercises exactly those areas.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prove that every possible emit type from the Claude stream is accounted for in a typed registry and can be rendered, while ensuring unknown types remain visible to operators rather than being silently dropped, and that the supervisor service propagates these declarations faithfully.
novelty: Combines unit/matrix tests with live supervisor HTTP acceptance and real captured stdout samples; uses an inline stub Claude binary to drive the supervisor through a full turn and inspects the resulting /watch stream and events.jsonl for gap-pressure telemetry.
contribution: A concrete acceptance harness that exercises the render-declaration registry, dispatch matrix, undeclared/fallback handling, and supervisor fan-out, including real-capture coverage validation and a stub-binary service-level scenario.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the R1.2 render-declaration layer that validates registry typing, event-to-render-key dispatch, real stream coverage, loud undeclared handling, and supervisor /watch fan-out using a stub Claude binary.
intention_for_embedding: To prove every documented and captured stream emit is declared and rendered, while guaranteeing unknown emits remain visible through drop hooks and service telemetry.

symbols.does: Assert a condition and count a passing check.
symbols.description: Takes a label and boolean condition; asserts the condition with a prefixed failure message, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints an ok line.

symbols.does: Validate registry loading, closed-vocab validation, and invalid-registry loud failure.
symbols.description: Loads the render-declaration registry and checks it contains expected core keys, then constructs an invalid registry with a bad placement value and verifies load_registry raises RegistryInvalid naming the offending entry and vocabulary.

symbols.does: Verify the dispatch matrix maps documented event shapes to correct render keys and renders them.
symbols.description: Iterates over MATRIX entries, declares each event, asserts the resulting render_key matches the expected key and that render_one returns truthy; also checks assistant block sub-dispatch including AskUserQuestion blocks_execution, user tool_result update_target, and loud field truncation.

symbols.does: Run real T2 stream captures and a sampled transcript corpus through the renderer.
symbols.description: Locates three real stdout captures under ~/xsession-tests, renders each with render_file, and asserts all events are declared and rendered; also samples up to 25 files from ~/.claude/projects and asserts zero undeclared events across the sample.

symbols.does: Exercise the loud undeclared and family-fallback render paths.
symbols.description: Registers a drop hook, declares a completely unknown emit type and verifies it renders as UnknownEvent with undeclared=true and that the hook records a gap entry; then declares an unseen system subtype and verifies it falls back to system/* with family_fallback and a soft drop.

symbols.does: Make an HTTP request to the test supervisor service.
symbols.description: Sends a JSON request to BASE+path with the given method and optional body, then returns the status code and parsed JSON response.

symbols.does: End-to-end supervisor test with a stub Claude binary and /watch stream inspection.
symbols.description: Writes an inline stub Claude script, starts session_supervisor on PORT, spawns a session, injects a user message, waits for one turn, reads the /watch stream to verify declared events for every stub emit including an unknown probe, checks the assistant sub-blocks, and polls events.jsonl for an agent_sessions.render_drop gap record.

symbols.does: Run all acceptance tests and print the pass tally.
symbols.description: Calls the five test functions in order and prints a PASS message with the total number of green checks; assertion failures are caught at module level and exit with code 1.
